mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal, with lower rates directly supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of July 2009 reflects post-crisis easing trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 9% triggers a regime shift where equity investors anticipate extended labor market weakness and reduced consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 9.0% in any month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in July 2009 but crisis-driven spike to 10%+ expected in coming months
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month window following UNRATE > 9% threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly decline by more than 5% month-over-month, signaling synchronized demand destruction across consumer and residential sectors. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day equity returns as forward GDP growth expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline >5% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1460K units in June 2009, reflecting post-crisis trough
regime_hint: demand-side recession with synchronized sector weakness
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the joint decline signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 3-month moving average by more than 1.5 percentage points, signaling a supply-side recovery that increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations. This trend mechanism operates over a 60-day horizon as production gains compound.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.57 in July 2009, recovering from crisis lows
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 52% of instances when INDPRO accelerates >1.5pp above 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth turns positive after 6+ consecutive months of decline, signaling labor market stabilization that restores consumer confidence and equity risk appetite. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns as forward earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth turns positive after 6+ months of consecutive decline
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157.7M in July 2009, near post-crisis trough
regime_hint: labor market stabilization in early recovery
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 18-month rolling window following the trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS stays above 3%, indicating monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation dynamics. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 5-day returns as markets anticipate policy error and growth headwinds.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 309.69 in July 2009, FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
regime_hint: monetary policy tightness relative to inflation backdrop
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1.5 million annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month, indicating synchronized demand recovery across residential and consumer sectors. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns as forward GDP growth accelerates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.5M annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1460K in June 2009, below trigger threshold
regime_hint: demand-side recovery with synchronized sector strength
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines more than 2% month-over-month, signaling supply-side contraction that compresses corporate profit margins and equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.57 in July 2009, recovering from crisis-driven declines
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window following the trigger event.
